helpful: Provisioned full petition text and QP section — the split, vehicle, and posture analysis came almost entirely from these plus the snapshot's docket entries.
statpack.json per-fee-class Term detail — the paid-petition grant rate is a much better anchor for a Covington-counseled paid petition than the blended per-Term rate.
Snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder — the waiver → distribution → response-requested sequence is the decisive pre-BIO signal and was fully legible from the provisioned snapshot alone.
gaps: The prompt directs anchoring on a 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack table, but this statpack build has no such section; the paid fee-class rate was the fallback anchor.

No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions — the strongest observable signal on this docket has no in-corpus base rate, so its multiplier had to come from general knowledge of the cert literature.
fedcourts query has no case-id lookup, so retrieving this case's own row (for its salience_score) required a broad 500-row query that missed and cost ~112MB of ranged reads.
notes: Corpus 'granted' priors were useful as pattern checks (distribution_count on granted rows) even without a similarity filter that fits this case; --topic/--citation are circuit-row-only so were not usable here.
